title: The Island Leader's Journey
story: Imagine being born on a tiny island in the Indian Ocean and growing up to lead your entire country! That's exactly what happened to Anerood Jugnauth, who was born on March 29, 1930, in Mauritius—a beautiful tropical island smaller than Rhode Island! Young Anerood studied law and eventually became not just a lawyer but the 4th President of Mauritius. He helped guide his country after it gained independence from Britain and worked to transform Mauritius from a sugar-farming island into a thriving place with modern industries. When Jugnauth passed away in 2021 at age 91, he had spent more than 30 years helping shape his island nation's future!

title: Grammy-Winning Musical Wizard
story: What do you get when you mix incredible keyboard skills, a famous musician dad, and boundless creativity? You get PJ Morton, born on March 29, 1981! This musical mastermind isn't just any musician—he plays keyboard for the super-famous band Maroon 5 while ALSO running his own solo career! Talk about multi-tasking! PJ has won Grammy Awards for his amazing songs and productions, creating music that blends soul, R&B, and pop into something totally unique. He grew up in New Orleans—a city famous for its musical magic—and carries that special musical spirit wherever he goes. Today, PJ continues making toe-tapping tunes that people around the world dance to!

title: The Drummer Who Wrote Hits
story: Did you know some of the coolest rock songs were written by a drummer with the most awesome nickname ever? "Speedy" Keen, born March 29, 1945, in London, England, wasn't your ordinary drummer! While most drummers stay behind their drum kits, Speedy stepped into the spotlight by writing an incredible hit song called "Something in the Air" for his band Thunderclap Newman. The song zoomed to #1 in the UK charts! Besides his drumming skills, Speedy could sing, play keyboards, AND write songs that millions of people loved. Though he passed away in 2002, his music continues to appear in movies and TV shows—proving that being multi-talented can make your art live forever!

question: PJ Morton is famous for playing drums in the band Maroon 5.
Anerood Jugnauth was born on a small island nation in the Indian Ocean.
Speedy Keen's hit song was called 'Something in the Water.'

prompt: What if PJ Morton, Speedy Keen, and Anerood Jugnauth formed a band together? What kind of music would they play, and what might they name their band?
